zh.Ouderkerk aan de Amstel, North Holland,: 荷蘭努-拉藍德武德可可 阿 德 阿姆斯特爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Gijsbrecht II van Amstel於1175年出生在荷蘭努-拉藍德武德可可 阿 德 阿姆斯特爾時，他的父親Gijsbrecht I van Amstel30歲，母親Hadewigis35歲。他和Bertrande van IJsselstein至少有1個兒子和2個女兒。他於1224年被列為ridder。他去世於大約1234年享年60歲。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Amstel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Mayer, Pinkus.

nameMeanings.name: Gijsbrecht
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erna, Franz, Reinold, Wilhelm.

German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements gis (from gīsil ‘pledge, hostage, noble offspring’; see Giesel ) + berht ‘bright, famous’, a cognate of Giselbert (see Gilbert ).

variants.name: Giesel
meanings: German: from the personal name Giesel, a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names with the first element gīsil ‘pledge, hostage, noble offspring’. In ancient and medieval ancient Germanic society, the younger children of kings and princes were sometimes sent to be brought up at the court of a neighboring ruler, as a pledge of peace between the two nations or clans. Thus, the male personal name Gisil and its feminine equivalent Gisela mean both ‘pledge’ and ‘noble offspring’.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Gijsbrecht II van Amstel was born in 1175, in Ouderkerk aan de Amstel, North Holland, Netherlands, his father, Gijsbrecht I van Amstel, was 30 and his mother, Hadewigis, was 35. He had at least 1 son and 2 daughters with Bertrande van IJsselstein. In 1224, at the age of 49, his occupation is listed as ridder. He died about 1234, at the age of 60.

fr.Ouderkerk aan de Amstel, North Holland,: Ouderkerk aan de Amstel, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gijsbrecht II van Amstel est né en 1175, à Ouderkerk aan de Amstel, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas, son père, Gijsbrecht I van Amstel, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Hadewigis, avait 35 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles avec Bertrande van IJsselstein. En 1224, à l’âge de 49 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est ridder. Il est décédé environ 1234, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.Ouderkerk aan de Amstel, North Holland,: Ouderkerk aan de Amstel, Nord-Holland, Niederlande
de.lifeSummary: Als Gijsbrecht II van Amstel 1175 in Ouderkerk aan de Amstel, Nord-Holland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gijsbrecht I van Amstel 30 und seine Mutter Hadewigis 35. Er hatte mit Bertrande van IJsselstein mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. 1224, als er 49 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als ridder aufgeführt. Er starb um 1234 im Alter von 60 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gijsbrecht II van Amstel è nato il nel 1175 nel Ouderkerk aan de Amstel, Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Gijsbrecht I van Amstel, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Hadewigis, aveva 35 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie con Bertrande van IJsselstein. Nel 1224, all'età di 49 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come ridder. Morì circa 1234, all'età di 60 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Gijsbrecht II van Amstelが1175年にオランダノオルド-hオランドオウデルケルク アーン ド アムステルで生まれた時，父Gijsbrecht I van Amstelは30歳，母Hadewigisは35歳であった。彼はBertrande van IJsselsteinとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。1224年，49歳の時，職業はridderと記録されている。彼は約1234年に60歳で死去した。

ko.Ouderkerk aan de Amstel, North Holland,: 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 우더커크 아아 데 아스텔
ko.lifeSummary: Gijsbrecht II van Amstel(이)가 1175년 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 우더커크 아아 데 아스텔에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gijsbrecht I van Amstel(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Hadewigis(은)는 35세였다. 그는 Bertrande van IJsselstein(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1224년, 49세의 나이로 그의 직업은 ridder이었다. 그는 대략 1234년 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери от Bertrande van IJsselstein. В 1224 году, в возрасте 49 лет,  он работал по профессии ridder. Он умер около 1234 г. в возрасте 60 лет.
